media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House

characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Tutter is a blue mouse, Pip and Pop are purple otters, Ojo is a red bear cub, and Treelo is a white, blue and green lemur. The only exception is Bear, who is brown. Black Bead Eyes: Bear, Ojo, Pip, and Pop have black eyes. Funny Animal: With the exception of Shadow, they're all animals that walk on two legs and talk. Interspecies Friendship: They're all close friends and honorary family, and only two pairs (Pip and Pop, and Bear and Ojo) are the same species. No Full Name Given: None of them have stated last names, except for Tutter, who,Depending on the Writer, might have a surname of "Tutter" or "the Mouse".

characters.name: Bear
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Beary Friendly: One of the most triumphant examples. He's a bear and one of the most amicable characters. Big Eater: Downplayed, but he enjoys his cheese drawer and he says that the meals are his favourite parts of the day in "Call it a Day". Big Fun: He's a large bear who's just as fun loving as the kids he lives with. Catchphrase: "What's that smell?! Oh, it's you! You know, you smell like X today. Or maybe you smell this good all the time." Deadpan Snarker: In a gentle, friendly way; he often shows a dry sense of humor (usually through making unamused expressions), which is played up in his appearances onThe Hollywood Squares.

characters.name: Tutter
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: A blue mouse who loves cheese and has a tendency to worry and fret (worry and fret, we tell ya!). Aerith and Bob: His name is not a real name, and his relatives have made-up names too, like Blotter and Flutter. His classmates, however, have real names, like Lily and Rita. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: A blue mouse. Everyone in his family is blue as well. Catchphrase:"Thank you, Bear!""It may be little to a big old bear like you, but to a mouse...""A mouse has to do things for himself in this world.""Oh, hickory dickory!" "It may be little to a big old bear like you, but to a mouse..."

characters.name: Treelo
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: HisSelf-Demonstrating Articlecan be foundhere Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Green with some white and blue in there. How many lemurs do you know that share his color scheme? Art Evolution: In Season 3, his puppet was updated to have functional eyelids, which make him more expressive. The Baby of the Bunch: Easily the most childish of the kids, being unaware of some concepts like snow, and having trouble speaking in full sentences. Catchphrase: Oddly enough, his catchphrase is his own name.

characters.name: Ojo
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: She pronounces Tutter's name "Tut-ter". Afraid of Needles: In one episode she hides in her room to avoid getting a shot until Bear helps her through it by singing a song about how they're over quickly. Age-Appropriate Angst: She's often seen getting sad about normal kiddie things, like wanting to be older/bigger and learning to use the toilet. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: She's a red-orange bear. Beary Friendly: Not as much as Bear, but still very friendly.

characters.name: Pip and Pop
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: Like Ojo, they pronounce Tutter's name "Tut-ter". Aerith and Bob: Pip is a real name, but Pop is not. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: A pair of purple otters. Big Brother Instinct:Downplayed, whenever Tutter is upset, they'll always be there to help him feel better. Black Bead Eyes: Both have these, although Pop's are slightly larger than Pip's.

characters.name: Luna
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Catchphrase:"I would love to, Bear.""Hello, Bear!""After all, I am the moon." "I would love to, Bear." "After all, I am the moon." Character Tics:She bounces around when singing.She tends to finish Bear's sentences. She bounces around when singing.

characters.name: Ray
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: The Face of the Sun: He has a face. Male Sun, Female Moon: He's the Male Sun to Luna's Female Moon. Meaningful Name: His name is Ray because suns have rays. Weird Sun: The sun can talk.

characters.name: Shadow
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Catchphrase:"When you need another story, just try and catch me!""Just watch!" before starting a story/song. "When you need another story, just try and catch me!" "Just watch!" before starting a story/song. Catchphrase Insult: Commonly calls Bear "you big old bear" or "you silly old bear". A Dog Named "Dog": A shadow named Shadow.

characters.name: Doc Hogg
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears only a pair of glasses. Crazy-Prepared: One episode shows that he always carries whipped cream around with him in case he needs it. The Medic: He's a medical doctor. Motor Mouth: He talks a lot. Often while other characters are talking. No Full Name Given: We don't know his first name.

characters.name: Jeremiah Tortoise
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Forgetful Jones: Jeremiah often forgets things and often stops in mid-sentence because he can't remember what it was that he was saying. Other times, he will repeat greet someone a second time after having just greeted them. Friendly Shopkeeper: He happily greets his customers and offers them free samples. Snail Mail: He is atortoisewho delivers the mail in Woodland Valley. You can see how this can go wrong. It's not unusual for stuff to take ages to arrive, and woe be those who get a wrong package because of his forgetfulness, forcing a redelivery. Species Surname: His last name is Tortoise.

characters.name: Grandma Flutter
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Adults Are More Anthropomorphic: She wears clothes, and unlike her grandson Tutter who's presumably a child, she never does anything particularly mouse-like. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: She's a blue mouse. Barefoot Cartoon Animal: She wears no shoes, but she does wear a dress and cardi. Birthday Episode: In "Grandma Flutter's 100th Birthday", she turns a hundred years old. Doting Grandparent: She loves both of her grandchildren and taught Tutter many things, including how to say thank you in Chinese and how to play sports.

characters.name: Blotter
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears nothing but a diaper. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Like her relatives, she's a blue mouse. Baby Talk: She can't talk yet, but she can babble. Prone to Tears: Being a baby, she cries a lot. Once when she needs her diaper changed, and once because she wanted to be played with. Sleepyhead: Justified - she's a baby, so she still takes naps.

characters.name: Christine
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Interspecies Friendship: A rabbit whose best friend is a bear.

characters.name: Etta and Otto
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Otto wears a hat and tie. Adults Are More Anthropomorphic: They wear clothes, while their grandsons go naked, and their granddaughter wears nothing but glasses. Alliterative Name: Implied for Otto; his granddaughter Tally is named Tally Otter, which could imply that his full name is Otto Otter. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: They're purple otters. Ambiguously Related: Pip and Pop are their grandsons, and Tally is presumably their granddaughter since she stayed with them once, but Pip and Pop's other cousin Rocko was never said to have any connection to them, leaving it unclear whether he's on Etta and Otto's side of the family or not.

characters.name: School Mice
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: The girl mice wear hairbows and Luke wears glasses. Adults Are More Anthropomorphic: The kids are all naked or mostly naked, and they messily eat cheese and don't have hair. Miss Martin has humanlike hair, wears full clothes, and doesn't particularly behave like a mouse. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Most of them are candy-coloured, with exceptions being Moss (black), Rita (grey), and Lily (brown). Class Clown: Moss is often cracking jokes. The Insomniac: "Tutter's First Sleepover Bash" shows that Luke can have trouble sleeping - he doesn't like sleeping with any kind of pillow other than a foam one, he wants a lullaby, and he almost considers going home before changing his mind.

characters.name: Jack
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Dogs Love Fire Hydrants: In the Season 4 intro, he sings that the town has hydrants. Funny Animal: He's an anthropomorphic dog.

characters.name: Skippy
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears sunglasses because he is blind. Funny Animal: He's an anthropomorphic squirrel. Interspecies Friendship: He's a squirrel who's friends with Treelo, a lemur. The Nose Knows: He has a strong sense of smell to compensate for his blindness.

characters.name: Harry
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Alliterative Family: He has a sister named Hallie. Artistic License – Ornithology: He can fly despite being a kid. Real ducks don't fly until they're grown. Funny Animal: He's an anthropomorphic duckling. Furry Reminder:Sometimes, he quacks.He can fly. Sometimes, he quacks.

characters.name: Tally Otter
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears nothing but glasses. Adults Are More Anthropomorphic: While not an adult, she is older than her little cousins, and unlike them, she's never shown doing anything particularly otter-like. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: She's blue-green. Funny Animal: Like her cousins, she's an anthropomorphic otter. Good with Numbers: Downplayed - her favourite subject in school is math, but being a young child, she's just learning addition and subtraction.

characters.name: Ursa
characters.media_title: Bear in the Big Blue House
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears a shawl. A Dog Named "Perro": Her name means "bear" in Latin. Funny Animal: Just like Bear, she is an anthropomorphic bear. Gratuitous Spanish: She greets Bear with "Hola!". Intergenerational Friendship: She has made friends with Ojo as of "You Never Know".
